response.justification: While the candidate brings valuable geospatial domain expertise through GISCollective and demonstrates experience with distributed systems at scale, there is a critical gap: the most recent C++ work ended in 2015, a decade ago, when C++ is the primary technology requirement. Recent career focus on web and application development (TypeScript, Node.js) rather than performance-critical systems, combined with no demonstrated navigation guidance or routing systems experience, makes this a poor fit for an immediate individual contributor role in a mission-critical routing pipeline.
strengths: Founded and maintains GISCollective, a production geospatial platform serving real users, demonstrating deep domain expertise in geographic data processing
Experienced operating distributed systems at scale—handled millions of HTTP requests per second at Dunnhumby with micro-services architecture, caching, and real-time data processing
Staff-level seniority with proven ability to learn across diverse technical stacks and philosophy of selecting the right tool for each problem
concerns: Critical C++ expertise gap—most recent C++ work ended at National Instruments in June 2015, over a decade ago, when the role requires modern C++ as primary requirement

Recent career trajectory focused on web and application development rather than performance-critical systems; no demonstrated experience optimizing latency-sensitive infrastructure or operating reliability-critical pipelines
No proven experience building navigation guidance or routing systems; GISCollective experience is in geospatial data collection and mapping, not vehicle routing or navigation tile generation
